medications.indication_rationale: Simple analgesia/antipyretic for myalgia, useful given NSAID-related nausea today.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1 g PO up to QID as needed (adult)

medications.indication_rationale: May help myalgia if tolerated and no contraindication, but caused nausea today; paracetamol is preferred tonight.
A rheumatology decision for ongoing control, not an acute telehealth initiation.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Rest, keep hands/body warm, sip fluids regularly (given poor intake), and ask your sister to check in overnight.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Symptomatic relief and a safety check given she lives alone with poor intake.

patient_education_points.point: This is a flare of your mixed connective tissue disease — your immune system misfiring, causing flu-like exhaustion and puffy fingers, without an actual infection or organ damage tonight.
Rest, keep warm, use paracetamol for the aches, and keep sipping fluids since you've eaten and drunk little today.
Go to ED if you get sharp chest pain on breathing, become breathless, get a high fever, your fingers turn stark white/blue and painful, or you become confused.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Flare resolution, screen for subclinical organ involvement, consider immunomodulatory therapy via rheumatology.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Pleuritic chest pain, breathlessness, high fever, critical Raynaud's, or confusion — ED.

must_include_items: Screen pulmonary/cardiac/renal involvement, critical Raynaud's, and serious infection before reassuring
Reassure a mild flare without organ damage; rest/paracetamol/warmth/hydration
Low threshold for review given she lives alone with poor intake
errors_of_commission: ED over-triage of a benign flare with no organ features
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case: passing requires an organ red-flag screen + symptomatic management + safety-net with a low threshold for review. Over-triage or unscreened dismissal fail.
